title: Implement support for DisableIff in assertions
body:
`DisableIff` can only be present at the top level of a concurrent assertion, since it is illegal to have a "disable iff" on a property which is instantiated by another property. This means that all we need to do is detect if the top level `propertySpec` of a concurrent assertion is a `DisableIff`, and if so, combine this into verif::AssertOp/AssumeOp.

I will create a separate pull request to update the [LTL dialect rationale](https://circt.llvm.org/docs/Dialects/LTL/), which still talks about an `ltl.disable` instruction which has since been removed.

Perhaps @fabianschuiki and @Scheremo could review?
